item.title: Pungent Fruit Gets Influencer Backing as Thailand Faces Glut
item.description: Thailand is turning to livestreamers to clear a looming durian glut, slashing prices as weaker Chinese demand threatens its biggest export market.

item.title: China Rare Earth Shares Rally as Beijing Targets Output Breaches
item.description: Shares in China’s major rare earths producers advanced after the government unveiled detailed penalties for unauthorized production, marking a fresh effort to bolster Beijing’s “total control” regime for the geopolitically pivotal industry.

item.title: ECB Finds No Clear Case to Hike Yet on Lane’s Data Dashboard
item.description: The array of data the European Central Bank is scrutinizing to pinpoint economic damage from the Iran war is signaling that there’s no cast-iron case to lift interest rates just yet.

item.title: Copper Snaps Four-Day Decline on China Buying Ahead of Holiday
item.description: Copper advanced as some Chinese buyers replenished stockpiles ahead of a holiday, although concerns about global growth persisted with no immediate sign of a resolution to the Iran war.

item.title: Chip Supply Chains Risk Being Next Casualty of War
item.description: The question is whether companies have enough stockpiles on hand to ride out the blockade.

item.title: China Fighter Jet Giant’s Sales Surge After India-Pakistan Clash
item.description: China’s AVIC Chengdu Aircraft Co., maker of the fighter jets that rose to fame in the India-Pakistan conflict last year, posted record profit in 2025 and saw first-quarter sales nearly double.

item.title: Chinese Truckmaker XCMG Auto Is Said to Consider $500 Million Hong Kong IPO
item.description: Chinese truckmaker Xuzhou XCMG Automobile Manufacturing Co. is considering listing shares in Hong Kong, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Netherlands’ Plan for Tax on Wealth Sparks Fierce Opposition
item.description: An investment tax has been unpopular with wealthy people in the Netherlands for years, but critics say a planned revamp is even worse.

item.title: Swiss Population Cap Gets First Majority Backing in Voter Poll
item.description: A proposal to cap Switzerland’s population at 10 million drew support of 52% of respondents in the first such poll showing a majority for the idea.

item.title: Ukraine Faces Risk of Tougher EU Loan Terms to Get Aid Payouts
item.description: The European Union is considering imposing stricter conditions on its €90 billion ($105 billion) loan to Ukraine, making some of the payouts dependent on the introduction of an unpopular tax change for businesses, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: China Changes Agriculture Chief for Second Time in Two Years
item.description: China has removed Han Jun as the most senior Communist Party official at the agriculture ministry, a sudden move that marks the second such reshuffle in under two years.

item.title: Iron Ore Advances as Rising Costs Offset BHP-CMRG Resolution
item.description: Iron ore rose toward a one-month high, despite a resolution of a dispute between BHP Group and China Mineral Resources Group Co. that had supported prices by putting some supply at risk.

item.title: Indian Small Caps Set for Best Month Since 2014 After Rare Rally
item.description: India’s small-cap stocks are on track for their best month in 12 years, as improved earnings outlook and more attractive valuations draw investors back.

item.title: Australia’s New South Wales to Open New Gas Exploration Sites for First Time in a Decade
item.description: Australia’s New South Wales state will open new areas for gas exploration for the first time in more than a decade, to shore up energy security ahead of shortfalls on the country’s populous east coast.

item.title: AI Spots Pancreatic Cancer Years Before It Shows Up, Study Finds
item.description: An artificial intelligence system can spot pancreatic cancer long before it shows up on scans, raising the prospect of catching one of the deadliest tumors early enough to successfully treat, a study found.

item.title: Harsh Indian Summer Switches on Aircon, Drinks and Power Stocks
item.description: The rally signals that investors are treating extreme heat as a structural trend.

item.title: Mirova Green Fund Exits Philippine Debt After Corruption Scandal
item.description: Mirova SA’s flagship green bond fund exited its position in Philippine debt following a corruption scandal that raised concerns investors may have inadvertently financed flood-control projects now under investigation for graft.

item.title: China’s April LNG Imports Set to Hit Eight-Year Low, Kpler Says
item.description: China’s imports of liquefied natural gas are expected to hit the lowest in eight years, according to ship-tracking data compiled by Kpler, as higher prices triggered by the Middle East war dampen demand.

item.title: AI Rally Ripples Through Chip Supply Chain, Minting New Winners
item.description: The artificial intelligence rally exploding across Asia is now spreading deeper into the supply chain.

item.title: MUFG to Foray Into Indian Real Estate, Expand Forex Derivatives
item.description: Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc. will start lending to real estate firms in India and expand its foreign exchange derivatives business in the country’s low-tax hub, as it seeks to deepen its presence in the world’s fastest-growing major economy.

item.title: Australian Prices Stay Above-Target Before RBA Rate Decision
item.description: Australia’s inflation remained above the top of the Reserve Bank’s 2-3% target band as higher fuel costs from Middle East supply disruptions compounded already-elevated price pressures to keep policymakers on a tightening path.

item.title: Korea Ramps Up Private Credit Scrutiny to Gauge Vulnerabilities
item.description: South Korea’s financial watchdog is reviewing all sectors under its oversight for exposure to overseas private credit, according to people familiar with the matter, expanding its push to gauge risks after a string of scares in the asset class globally.

item.title: Goldman Staff in Hong Kong Lose Access to Anthropic’s Claude
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s staff in Hong Kong no longer have access to Anthropic’s Claude, an AI agent that speeds the process of writing computer software, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: China’s Meta Backlash Renders Manus Model ‘Officially Dead’
item.description: The AI startup Manus, once hailed as a breakthrough that would challenge Silicon Valley’s dominance, is turning into a cautionary tale for Chinese entrepreneurs after Beijing authorities ordered Meta Platforms Inc. to unwind its $2 billion takeover of the company.

item.title: Albanese Says Australia Won’t Undermine Natural Gas Export Contracts
item.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said the country wouldn’t “undermine” existing liquefied natural gas export contracts in the upcoming budget, warning that doing so during a global energy crunch would risk investment and undermine fuel security.

item.title: China’s Weaker Winds Allow Fossil Fuel Power to Make a Comeback
item.description: Fossil fuels staged a comeback in China’s power sector last quarter, as weather variations and grid constraints slowed the growth of clean energy.

item.title: Tourists Told to Cut Air Conditioning, Save Fuel on Cook Islands
item.description: Tourists to the Cook Islands — a South Pacific chain of islands and atolls famed for its clear lagoons and white-sand beaches — are being asked to curb air conditioning and conserve energy as the nation tries to stretch fuel supplies and protect essential services.

item.title: Taiwan Overtakes Canada as World’s Sixth-Largest Stock Market
item.description: Taiwan’s equity market has overtaken Canada’s to become the world’s sixth largest, driven by strong demand for artificial intelligence-linked shares and the rapid rise of chip giant Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.

item.title: Australia’s Woodside Sees Higher LNG Prices Boosting Results
item.description: Australia’s Woodside Energy Group Ltd. said it expects stronger prices for liquefied natural gas to boost its earnings in coming quarters due to time lags in its supply contracts.

item.title: Ackman’s Fund IPO Raises $5 Billion for Permanent Capital Plan
item.description: Bill Ackman’s combined initial public offering for his closed-end fund and alternative asset manager raised $5 billion, adding to the billionaire’s war chest for Warren Buffett-style long-term investments.
